content.problemStatement:
There are NNN caves in a row, numbered 111 through NNN. For each valid iii, the radiation power in the iii-th cave is CiC_iCi‚Äã. Originally, the radiation level in each cave was 000. Then, for each valid iii, the radiation power in cave iii increased the radiation levels in the caves i‚àíCi,‚Ä¶,i+Cii-C_i, \ldots, i+C_ii‚àíCi‚Äã,‚Ä¶,i+Ci‚Äã inclusive (if they exist) by 111, so all the caves are radioactive now.

Radiation is not the only problem, though. There are also NNN zombies with health levels H1,H2,‚Ä¶,HNH_1, H_2, \ldots, H_NH1‚Äã,H2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,HN‚Äã. You want to kill all of them by getting them to the caves in such a way that there is exactly one zombie in each cave. A zombie dies in a cave if and only if the radiation level in that cave is equal to the health level of the zombie. Is it possible to kill all the zombies?
constraints: 1‚â§Ci,Hi‚â§1091 \le C_i, H_i \le 10^91‚â§Ci‚Äã,Hi‚Äã‚â§109 for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers C1,C2,‚Ä¶,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1‚Äã,C2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,CN‚Äã.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers H1,H2,‚Ä¶,HNH_1, H_2, \ldots, H_NH1‚Äã,H2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,HN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the string `"YES"` if it is possible to kill all the zombies or `"NO"` if it is impossible (without quotes).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In both example test cases, the final radiation levels in the caves are (5,5,4,4,3)(5, 5, 4, 4, 3)(5,5,4,4,3). For example, the radiation power in cave 111 increased the radiation levels in caves 111 and 222 (there is no cave 000) by 111, and the radiation power in cave 444 increased the radiation levels in all caves by 111.
